story.iWant: Recipe to verify that Capture One .costyle files can be round-trip converted (costyle → UniversalRecipe → costyle) with 95%+ accuracy
story.soThat: I can trust that my preset adjustments will be preserved when converting between formats, and I have confidence in the conversion quality before using Recipe in production workflows

subtasks.subtask: Implement round-trip test flow: Parse → Generate → Parse → Compare
Implement compareRecipes() helper function with parameter-by-parameter comparison
Run round-trip test on each sample file
Create compareParameter() functions for different parameter types
Exposure: Exact match within float precision
Contrast, Saturation, Tint, Clarity: Within ±1 integer value
Count matched vs total parameters, return accuracy percentage
Generate parameter breakdown report with AccuracyReport struct
Document parameters not representable in UniversalRecipe
Update parameter-mapping.md with round-trip accuracy notes
Install Capture One Pro trial version
Generate 3 test .costyle files from round-trip conversion
Load original .costyle in Capture One and take screenshots
Load round-trip .costyle in Capture One and take screenshots
Ensure round-trip tests run in CI
Update README.md with round-trip accuracy claims
Add round-trip test example to testdata/costyle/README.md

criterion.title: Implement Round-Trip Test Suite
criterion.requirement: Create automated round-trip test function in costyle_test.go
Test flow: Parse .costyle → UniversalRecipe → Generate .costyle → Parse → Compare
Verify all core parameters preserved with 95%+ accuracy
Test with minimum 5 real-world .costyle samples from Etsy/marketplaces
Include edge cases: extreme values, minimal presets, complex adjustments
All round-trip tests pass in CI

criterion.title: Verify Key Adjustments Preserved Exactly
criterion.requirement: Exposure: No precision loss (exact float equality)
Contrast: Within ±1 integer value (acceptable precision loss from float→int→float)
Saturation: Within ±1 integer value
Temperature: Within ±2 units (acceptable given Kelvin conversion)
Tint: Within ±1 integer value
Clarity: Within ±1 integer value
Test verifies each parameter independently

criterion.title: Document Lossy Conversions
criterion.requirement: Identify parameters not representable in UniversalRecipe
Document expected precision loss (float→int conversions)
Create known-conversion-limitations.md in docs/
List parameters skipped during conversion (local adjustments, layers, masking)
Provide examples of acceptable vs. unacceptable precision loss
Update parameter-mapping.md with round-trip accuracy notes

criterion.title: Visual Validation in Capture One
criterion.requirement: Generate 3 test .costyle files from round-trip conversion
Load generated files in Capture One Pro (trial version)
Apply to test images (portrait, landscape, product)
Visual spot-check: Verify adjustments render correctly
Compare side-by-side with original .costyle output
Document validation results in test report

criterion.title: Measure and Report Accuracy Metrics
criterion.requirement: Calculate accuracy percentage for each test file
Report metrics: min/max/average accuracy across all parameters
Target: 95%+ average accuracy across test suite
Generate accuracy report (JSON or markdown format)
Include parameter-by-parameter breakdown
Fail test if accuracy < 95% (hard requirement)

criterion.title: Test Coverage for Round-Trip Paths
criterion.requirement: Unit tests: TestRoundTrip_Costyle() for single .costyle file
Batch tests: TestRoundTrip_Costylepack() for .costylepack bundles
Edge case tests: Empty recipe, extreme values, missing parameters
Cross-format tests: .costyle → .xmp → .costyle (verify no corruption)
Test coverage ≥85% for round-trip test code
All tests pass in CI

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support
doc.snippet: Round-trip conversion preserves 95%+ of parameter values. Key adjustments (exposure, contrast, saturation) preserved exactly (no precision loss). Document known limitations of lossy conversions.

doc.title: Epic Technical Specification: Capture One Format Support
doc.snippet: Round-trip test pattern: Parse → Generate → Parse → Compare. Tolerance: ±1 for integer rounding. Coverage target: ≥85% for round-trip test code.

doc.title: Parameter Mapping Rules
doc.snippet: Recipe achieves ≥95% visual similarity for most conversions using weighted parameter comparison with tolerance thresholds. Exposure weight: 1.0, Contrast weight: 1.0, Saturation weight: 0.8. Integer tolerance: ±1, Float tolerance: ±0.01.

doc.title: Known Conversion Limitations
doc.snippet: Recipe achieves 95%+ visual similarity for most conversions, but some features are unmappable. Document provides feature-by-feature breakdown with visual impact assessment (Delta E, SSIM) and workarounds.

doc.title: Story 8.1: Capture One Parser
doc.snippet: Parse() function extracts exposure, contrast, saturation, temperature, tint, clarity from Capture One .costyle XML. Returns *models.UniversalRecipe.

doc.title: Story 8.2: Capture One Generator
doc.snippet: Generate() function creates .costyle XML from UniversalRecipe. Maps parameters with range scaling. Returns formatted XML bytes.

doc.title: Story 8.3: Capture One Pack Support
doc.snippet: Unpack() and Pack() functions handle .costylepack ZIP bundles. Parse multiple .costyle files from archive. Performance target: < 5 seconds for 50-file bundles.

artifact.reason: Parse .costyle XML to UniversalRecipe - used in round-trip test flow (step 1 and 3)
Generate .costyle XML from UniversalRecipe - used in round-trip test flow (step 2)
Existing round-trip comparison function - reference pattern for costyle round-trip tests. Implements tolerance-based parameter comparison with weights.
Example of limited comparison for formats with unmappable parameters - reference for handling Capture One limitations.
NP3 round-trip test pattern - reference implementation showing file discovery, Parse → Generate → Parse flow, and parametersMatch() helper.
Parameter comparison with tolerance - reference for implementing compareRecipes() for costyle tests.
Lrtemplate round-trip test - another reference pattern showing successful round-trip validation with compareRecipes() helper.
XMP round-trip tests - reference for XML-based format round-trip testing (similar to .costyle XML structure).
Core data model for all conversions - defines parameters to compare in round-trip tests.

go.package: XML parsing for .costyle files (via costyle package)
ZIP handling for .costylepack bundles (via costyle package)
File path handling for test discovery

constraints.constraint: Round-trip accuracy must be ≥95% average across all test files
Exposure: exact match (within float precision ±0.01). Integer parameters: ±1. Temperature: ±2 Kelvin.
Test coverage ≥85% for round-trip test code
Round-trip test suite must complete within CI timeout (10 minutes)
Minimum 5 real-world .costyle samples required for representative testing
All lossy conversions must be documented in known-conversion-limitations.md

interface.name: compareRecipes
interface.signature: func compareRecipes(t *testing.T, orig, final *models.UniversalRecipe, tolerance int)

tests.standards: Recipe uses Go's standard testing package with table-driven tests. Round-trip tests follow the pattern: (1) Load original file, (2) Parse to UniversalRecipe, (3) Generate target format, (4) Parse generated output, (5) Compare with tolerance. Test coverage target is ≥85%. All tests must pass in CI. Tests use t.Parallel() for performance. Helper functions use t.Helper() for clean stack traces.

locations.location: testdata/costyle/ - Test fixtures and sample .costyle files
testdata/costyle/real-world/ - Real-world preset samples from Etsy/marketplaces

ideas.idea: TestRoundTrip_Costyle: Test single .costyle file round-trip with 5+ real samples. Load testdata/costyle/real-world/*.costyle, run Parse → Generate → Parse, verify accuracy ≥95%.
TestParameterAccuracy: Verify each parameter independently with appropriate tolerance. Exposure: ±0.01, Contrast/Saturation/Tint/Clarity: ±1, Temperature: ±2.
TestEdgeCases: Empty recipe (all zeros), extreme values (exposure +2.0, contrast +100), minimal recipe (only exposure set), complex recipe (all parameters populated).
TestAccuracyMetrics: Calculate min/max/avg accuracy across all samples. Generate JSON report with parameter breakdown. Assert avg ≥ 95%.
TestRoundTrip_Costylepack: Test .costylepack bundle round-trip. Unpack → Convert recipes → Pack → Unpack → Compare each recipe pair.
TestRoundTrip_CrossFormat: Test .costyle → .xmp → .costyle and .costyle → .np3 → .costyle to verify no corruption through intermediate formats.
Benchmark tests: BenchmarkRoundTrip_Costyle to measure performance. Target: < 100ms per file.
